audienceSections.normal: In a description of Anna’s room, you will use [[grammar:в + предложный падеж]]. Use [[grammar:в]] with the location form of a noun to mean ‘in.’ В комнате means ‘in the room’; комната changes to комнате. Do not keep the dictionary ending after в in this location.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:в + предложный падеж]] in a description of Anna’s room. For static location, preposition в selects the prepositional case. Existential есть introduces a present entity, while находится lexically predicates location.

audienceSections.normal: Use [[grammar:в]] with the location form of a noun to mean ‘in.’ В комнате means ‘in the room’; комната changes to комнате. Do not keep the dictionary ending after в in this location.
audienceSections.expert: For static location, preposition в selects the prepositional case. Existential есть introduces a present entity, while находится lexically predicates location.

audienceSections.normal: Keep Cyrillic as the main text. In the reading help, an acute accent marks the stressed vowel; the accent is a study mark and is not normally written in everyday Russian.
audienceSections.expert: The support uses Cyrillic with a single acute accent on the stressed vowel of polysyllabic content words. It introduces no romanization and leaves monosyllables unmarked unless contrast requires it.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact russian line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:в + предложный падеж]] where it appears.

- Это комната Анны. — This is Anna's room.
- Комната находится в доме. — The room is in the house.
- В комнате есть стол. — There is a table in the room.
- На столе лежит книга. — A book lies on the table.
- Рядом стоит стул. — A chair stands nearby.
- В комнате есть окно. — There is a window in the room.
breakdown.expert:
